candidate_llm_application: BOMA-RING-KV: 3-tier concentric KV-cache memory hierarchy organized by attention-importance and role rather than recency. Inner ring R_in stores top-K_in KVs by cumulative attention score (never evicted); middle ring R_mid stores mid-percentile KVs (evictable on miss); outer ring R_out stores fixed system-prompt/safety/retrieval-guard tokens (defensive boundary, accessed last). Retrieval traverses R_in to R_mid to R_out with early exit.

results.title: Reformulating KV Cache Eviction Long-Context LLM (H2O/Scissorhands family)
results.rationale: H2O/Scissorhands heavy-hitter eviction with 'consistently-important entries never evicted' is precisely the inner-ring-highest-attention-never-evict mechanism that defines R_in of BOMA-RING-KV; same form (attention-importance KV retention) and same functional effect (top-K attention KVs are protected from eviction).

results.title: DefensiveKV Taming Fragility KV Cache Eviction
results.rationale: DefensiveKV explicitly preserves safety-critical KV entries as a defensive boundary against eviction, which is exactly the outer-ring R_out 'safety/system-prompt/retrieval-guard tokens (defensive boundary)' role in BOMA-RING-KV; semantic and functional alignment on the safety-tier component is high.

results.title: SafeKV Safe KV-Cache Sharing LLM Serving
results.rationale: SafeKV's snippet explicitly describes 'outer-ring defense layer' with sensitivity-based separation of sensitive vs non-sensitive entries; this is verbatim the outer-ring-safety-guard-tokens design of BOMA-RING-KV and also constitutes a priority tier, so multiple kw matches plus high sem/func.

results.title: Cache What Lasts Token Retention Memory-Bounded KV Cache (TRIM-KV)
results.rationale: TRIM-KV uses a learnable retention gate with a decay-over-time scalar score under a memory budget; this is a single-tier value-based retention without concentric rings, a safety boundary, or inner-to-outer traversal, so the form is only loosely related.

results.title: LMCache Hot/Warm/Cold Hierarchical KV Tier
results.rationale: LMCache provides a hot/warm/cold three-tier KV hierarchy with priority-based access that is the functional analog of inner/middle/outer rings; although LMCache tiers by recency/locality rather than attention percentile, the candidate's own diff statement concedes a multi-tier KV hierarchy with priority access already exists, putting form and function above 0.7.

results.title: Tiered KV Cache on Google Kubernetes Engine
results.rationale: Google's tiered KV cache on GKE delivers multi-tier KV with priority-aware access patterns, hitting the priority-tier form at the 0.70 boundary; it lacks the attention-importance percentile and safety-boundary specifics but the concentric-ring/priority-tier semantic similarity meets threshold.

results.title: CTkvr Centroid-then-Token KV Cache Retrieval (2025/12)
results.rationale: CTkvr's snippet states it covers 'concentric retrieval rings' via two-stage centroid->token retrieval; both the concentric-ring vocabulary and the inner-to-outer (coarse->fine) traversal order are anticipated, giving 2 kw matches and high sem/func.

results.title: Pitfalls of KV Cache Compression
results.rationale: The paper observes that KV cache compression disproportionately targets defense (safety) entries and motivates SafeKV-style mitigation; this is a critique surveying the same defense-tier concept but does not itself propose a concentric ring or a never-evict inner ring, so it lands just below thresholds.

reason: Six of eight results trigger hits and the three core components of BOMA-RING-KV are each independently anticipated by prior work: (a) the inner-ring 'highest-attention never-evict' tier is exactly H2O/Scissorhands heavy-hitter retention (2605.07234, sem 0.82, func 0.80, kw 2); (b) the outer-ring 'safety/defense' tier is DefensiveKV (2510.13334, sem 0.78, func 0.78) and SafeKV (jhDsbd5eXL, kw 2, sem 0.80, func 0.78), with SafeKV's own description even using the phrase 'outer-ring defense layer'; (c) the multi-tier priority-access hierarchy with concentric/coarse-to-fine traversal is LMCache (kw 2, sem 0.74, func 0.72), CTkvr (kw 2, sem 0.76, func 0.74), and Google Tiered KV (sem 0.70). Three distinct effect clusters (importance-never-evict, safety-boundary, concentric-priority-tier) all clear threshold, so the recombination is densely covered. Even the candidate's own differentiation statement names LMCache, IMPRESS, CTkvr, and Google Tiered KV as existing hierarchical KV work, and the search results confirm the defense-tier component is independently established by DefensiveKV/SafeKV.
recommendation: Pivot the novelty axis: (1) do not rely on the concentric-ring metaphor or on combining attention-importance + safety-boundary + multi-tier - all three are already individually published; (2) consider a sharper mechanism such as cross-ring token migration policies (promotion/demotion triggers tied to entropy/uncertainty rather than attention), or a learned ring-assignment controller that adapts K_in per query; (3) alternatively shift to a form less occupied in 2025-2026 KV-cache literature (e.g., dual-pointer ring with adversarial demotion, or per-head ring topology where each attention head has its own R_in/R_out) where the eight-result coverage would be thinner.
